title: Change-detection magnitude + cooldown gates for curmudgeon Priority 3
problem: Curmudgeon Priority 3 (change-driven scan) currently re-reviews any item whose fingerprint fields moved by >20%, with no cooldown and no cumulative-magnitude check. This produces cycle-5 / cycle-6 reviews driven by small prose polish edits. Concrete evidence in the last ~2 weeks of reviews/*.c*.json (retroactive analysis below): 22 change-detected reviews, 5 of which fired within 48h of the prior cycle. WIN-032 hit c6 only 3.0 hours after c5 with ~0% text delta — the new review found minor-only holes, redundant with c5. WIN-008.c4 fired 2h after c3 with a 37% detail_evidence_length delta that was pure verdict-phrasing tweak, not substantive. These thrash runs consume Opus cycles while the pending-digest's 200-entry severity_breakdown has zero critical/major entries — meaningful adversarial capacity is being spent on noise.

representative_thrash_cases.verdict_of_this_run: pure noise — 3h after c5, zero substantive text movement, only minor phrasing finding
2h after c3, verdict-phrasing edit, nothing new
10.5h after c3, verdict length moved but nothing substantive in findings
5.5h after c4; finding was re-statement of c4's major finding, not a new one. A cumulative fingerprint update at 48h expiry would have caught it with a single Opus run instead of two.
Correctly fires under relaxed cooldown because prior was critical (cooldown=12h, elapsed 18.5h). Gate should NOT skip this case.

evidence.detection_query: python3 scan of monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.c*.json grouping by target_id, comparing each change-detected review's text_fingerprint fields against prior cycle's, and computing hours_since_last from reviewed_at timestamps. Full script in appendix.

proposed_fix.summary: Add magnitude + cooldown gates to the Priority 3 change-detection procedure (curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md). Candidates still flag per the current >20%/verdict-changed rules, but a compound gate suppresses review when cumulative text delta is small AND the prior cycle is recent (48h default, 12h if prior max severity was critical/major). Skipped candidates DO NOT reset the stored fingerprint, so accumulated drift eventually fires. Verdict-value changes always bypass the gate. A one-line-per-skip ledger (skip-log.jsonl) lets tinker audit the gate monthly and retune thresholds.

files_to_create.purpose: Append-only ledger of change-driven skip decisions. Created empty; curmudgeon appends one JSON object per skip. Tinker reads during Mode 3 cost-engineering to verify no high-severity findings are being suppressed and to retune thresholds if the skip rate drifts. Format: {"timestamp":"ISO","target_id":"WIN-NNN","reason":"magnitude|cooldown|both","total_char_delta":float,"hours_since_last":float,"prev_severity":"critical|major|moderate|minor|none","cooldown_hours":int,"in_shadow_period":bool}

files_to_modify.find:
3. **Flag changed items.** An item needs re-review if:
   - Any text field length changed by >20%
   - The `verdict` field is different
   - The item has no prior review at all (newly added)
   - A `tldr_evidence` or `tldr_verdict` was added or substantially changed

4. **Prioritize.** Among flagged items, prefer: verdict changes > large text rewrites > new items > TLDR-only changes. If multiple items tie, prefer WINs over sections (WINs are what readers see first).
files_to_modify.replace:
3. **Flag candidates for re-review.** An item is a *candidate* for re-review if:
   - Any text field length changed by >20%
   - The `verdict` field value is different
   - The item has no prior review at all (newly added)
   - A `tldr_evidence` or `tldr_verdict` was added or substantially changed

3b. **Apply change-driven gates (magnitude + cooldown).** Before adding a flagged candidate to the review list, apply the following compound gate. This prevents micro-edit thrash while preserving detection of substantive or accumulated change. The gate applies ONLY to change-driven candidates — items in the priority queue (Step 0b), pending human notes (Step 0c), major-change audits (Step 0c2), holistic reviews (Priority 4), and spot-checks (Priority 5) are never gated.

   **Verdict-value bypass.** If the `verdict` field *value* (not just its length) differs from the stored fingerprint, ALWAYS review — verdict flips are high-signal and must never be suppressed. Skip the rest of the gate.

   **Magnitude measurement.** Compute `total_char_delta = |sum(current_field_lengths) - sum(stored_field_lengths)| / max(1, sum(stored_field_lengths))`. Fields: `claim_length`, `finding_length`, `detail_evidence_length`, `detail_verdict_length`. A candidate is *magnitude-small* iff `total_char_delta < 0.10` (cumulative drift below 10%).

   **Cooldown measurement.** Compute `hours_since_last = (now - prior review's reviewed_at) / 3600`. Set `cooldown_hours = 12` if the prior review's maximum hole severity was `critical` or `major`; else `cooldown_hours = 48`.

   **Gate decision.** SKIP the candidate iff `hours_since_last < cooldown_hours` AND `total_char_delta < 0.10`. Otherwise proceed to review.

   **Fingerprint carry-forward (critical correctness rule).** When a candidate is skipped by this gate, DO NOT update the stored fingerprint. The stored fingerprint must continue to reflect the last actually-reviewed cycle. This way, many small edits accumulate against the original baseline, and when the cooldown expires OR the cumulative magnitude crosses 10%, a review fires against the *accumulated* change — not against the last micro-edit. If you write a review, then update the fingerprint as normal.

   **Shadow period (first 7 days after this gate lands).** During the shadow period, log the would-skip decision to `monitor/curmudgeon/skip-log.jsonl` with `in_shadow_period: true` but STILL proceed to review. This lets tinker audit what would have been suppressed before the gate takes effect. After 7 days, tinker closes the shadow period (see PROP-008 migration section) and the gate becomes live.

   **Skip logging.** Every gate decision (both shadow-period would-skips and live skips) appends one JSON line to `monitor/curmudgeon/skip-log.jsonl`:

   ```json
   {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T12:34:56Z","target_id":"WIN-032","reason":"cooldown","total_char_delta":0.043,"hours_since_last":3.0,"prev_severity":"major","cooldown_hours":12,"in_shadow_period":false}
   ```

   `reason` is `"magnitude"` if only the magnitude test passed to skip, `"cooldown"` if only the cooldown test, `"both"` when both were skip-positive (this is the common case — skip requires both).

4. **Prioritize.** Among non-skipped flagged candidates, prefer: verdict changes > large text rewrites > new items > TLDR-only changes. If multiple items tie, prefer WINs over sections (WINs are what readers see first).
files_to_modify.explanation: Inserts Step 3b between the current Step 3 (flag) and Step 4 (prioritize). The gate applies cumulative-magnitude (10% total char) AND cooldown (48h default / 12h if prev critical/major) in an AND combination, with fingerprint carry-forward so accumulated small edits eventually fire a single review against the full delta. A 7-day shadow period (tracked via in_shadow_period flag in the skip-log) lets us verify no high-severity findings would be suppressed before the gate goes live.

reasoning.why_not_operator_suggested_5_percent_cha: Operator proposed 5% char / 3% word. Retroactive test against the 22-review sample: 5% char alone catches only 1/22 thrash cases (WIN-032.c6). The thrash is dominated not by tiny deltas but by repeated fires on moderately-changed text within a short window. A 10% total-char magnitude threshold is a better match to the empirical noise floor and leaves 20% per-field unchanged (that rule still FIRES the candidate; the gate is a second stage that suppresses if BOTH small AND recent).
reasoning.why_not_word_delta: Char delta correlates tightly with word delta in this dataset; adding a second dimension gives no extra catches. Keeping the gate one-dimensional is simpler and easier for tinker to audit in the skip-log.
reasoning.why_48h_default_cooldown: Matches operator suggestion. Retroactive analysis: 4/5 within-48h thrash reviews would be skipped; hours_since_last distribution shows 77% of change-detected reviews fire >48h after the prior cycle (and are therefore untouched). 48h preserves the slow-drift detection that Priority 3 exists for.
reasoning.why_12h_for_critical_or_major: Matches operator suggestion. If prior cycle flagged critical/major holes, we want a faster re-check to catch patch completeness. WIN-068.c5 (18.5h after c4 critical, correctly allowed) is the canonical case. The 12h window still skips WIN-032.c5 (5.5h after c4 major) and WIN-032.c6 (3h after c5 major) — both empirically redundant.
reasoning.why_verdict_bypass: Verdict flips are the highest-signal change category. Suppressing a verdict-flip review to save an Opus run would invert the cost tradeoff — the whole curmudgeon exists to catch these.

retroactive_skip_rate.false_negative_rate: 0/22 — zero cases where the gate would suppress a novel critical/major finding

tradeoffs: What we gain: ~4 Opus runs saved per 2-week window (~2 Opus runs/week), cleaner priority-queue attention, less pending-digest clutter from near-duplicate cycle reviews. What we might lose: (a) occasional serendipity where a within-cooldown review catches a regression from a parallel patch. Mitigation: fingerprint_carry_forward ensures the regression still fires at cooldown expiry against accumulated delta. (b) longer time-to-detection for a near-verdict-threshold hole that falls just below the 10% cumulative. Mitigation: 48h is not long in the scale of this project; the priority queue path (decider-pushed) bypasses the gate for any decider-triggered post-patch review. (c) added complexity in curmudgeon.md — this is the biggest cost, offset by moving the details to the reference file rather than the dispatcher.
estimated_impact: ~2 Opus runs/week saved at current cadence. More importantly: Part 2b's adversarial bandwidth shifts to analyst-drafted expansions (the EXP-183 / SEC-4.1 / SEC-2b.5 kind of work), which currently compete for the same review capacity. Qualitative gain larger than the token savings.

phase_1_shadow.behavior: Gate logic runs; would-skip decisions log to skip-log.jsonl with in_shadow_period:true; the review STILL proceeds. No suppression yet.
phase_1_shadow.success_criteria: At the end of 7 days, tinker audits skip-log.jsonl: (a) confirm zero would-skip reviews produced a novel critical or major finding that was NOT already present in the prior cycle's holes_found; (b) confirm skip rate is in the 15–25% range (matches retroactive estimate); (c) confirm no verdict-value changes appear in the would-skip set (would-skip ∩ verdict_changed should be empty by construction — if any appear, the bypass logic is wrong).

phase_2_enforce.trigger: Tinker Mode 3 run after shadow period completes, issues a PROP-008-ENFORCE patch that flips in_shadow_period default to false.

rollback_plan.trigger: If phase 1 audit shows novel critical/major findings in the would-skip set, OR the skip rate exceeds 30% (evidence the gate is too aggressive).
rollback_plan.action: Revert the files_to_modify patch (single block replacement). The skip-log.jsonl can stay as a historical artifact or be archived to monitor/tinker/archive/. No data loss — fingerprints were carry-forward preserved throughout shadow period, so reverting leaves the tracker in a consistent state.

migration_plan.monitoring: Tinker Mode 3 should scan skip-log.jsonl monthly and report: total skip count, skip rate, distribution of reasons (magnitude vs cooldown vs both), and any shadow-period would-skip entries with current-cycle max_severity ≥ major. If any appear, raise them as findings.

non_goals: Do NOT touch priority-queue.json — that mechanism works.
Do NOT touch Step 0c (human notes) or Step 0c2 (major-change audit) — orthogonal.
Do NOT remove change-detection — the pattern is valuable for slow-drift detection; we're only suppressing thrash-level redundancy.
why_human_needed: Thresholds (10% char, 48h/12h cooldown) are tuned against a 22-review retroactive sample; operator should confirm these match their intent before the prompt patch is applied. Also: operator must approve the shadow-period design (7 days) vs. immediate-enforce.
verification_pattern: grep -q 'Apply change-driven gates' monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md && grep -q 'Fingerprint carry-forward' monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
completion_criteria: Operator reviews the PROP. Approves thresholds + shadow-period design, or revises them. Decider (or human via clone) applies the files_to_modify patch and creates the skip-log.jsonl empty file. Tinker Mode 3 picks up skip-log.jsonl monitoring on next run.

references.retroactive_analysis_script: Python AST over monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.c*.json — see evidence.detection_query; results in evidence.representative_thrash_cases and threshold_calibration.retroactive_skip_rate
references.related_pipeline_signal: pending-digest.json severity_breakdown shows zero critical/major across 200 tracked reviews — confirming current thrash is high-volume, low-severity
